objective: Open the governance execution epic and downstream child issue map for the approved thin session-contract adapter rollout, mirror the issue-backed execution contract in governance artifacts, and keep the branch planning-only while downstream repo mutations remain separate child slices.
scope_boundary: Create governance epic #579 and the downstream child issue map.
Record repo-specific acceptance criteria for each downstream child issue.
Update governance backlog/progress mirrors for the open epic.
Capture review, validation, and closeout evidence for the epic-opening slice itself.
Keep downstream repo execution outside this branch.
out_of_scope: Direct edits to local-ai-machine, ai-integration-services, seek-and-ponder, knocktracker, Stampede, HealthcarePlatform, or ASC-Evaluator in this branch.
Reopening governance-source SSOT decisions settled by issue #576.
Adding repos outside the approved rollout wave order.
Expanding consumer repos into thick local governance manuals.
research_summary: Issue #575 approved the thin session-contract adapter rollout map and issue #576 merged the governance-source SSOT reconciliation. The remaining missing step was the execution tracker itself: a governance epic and downstream child issue set with repo-specific acceptance criteria. Prior rollout epics such as #439 and #507 used a governance epic plus explicit child stories. This slice applies that same pattern to the thin-adapter rollout so downstream repo work proceeds through named lanes instead of ad hoc reminders.

sprints.name: Epic and child issue creation
sprints.goal: Create the governance execution epic and the downstream child issue map in the approved rollout order.
tasks: Open governance epic #579 with epic-level acceptance criteria.
Open repo-native child issues for all seven governed repos in wave order.
Update the epic body with the concrete downstream issue links.
acceptance_criteria: Epic #579 exists and lists all seven downstream child issues.
The downstream child issues exist in the correct repos.
The child issue set matches the rollout order approved in issue #575.

sprints.name: Repo-specific acceptance criteria
sprints.goal: Ensure each downstream issue has enforceable repo-specific acceptance criteria rather than generic rollout text.
tasks: Bind thin-adapter, SSOT-pointer, and tracked-versus-local wiring expectations into each child issue.
Record repo-specific preservation constraints such as riskfix lanes, HIPAA/PII floors, and knowledge-repo exceptions.
acceptance_criteria: Every child issue requires thin CLAUDE.md/CODEX.md adapters that point to governance SSOT surfaces.
Every child issue prohibits conflicting operator-facing bootstrap/review paths.
Repo-specific constraints are preserved where applicable.

sprints.name: Governance mirror and evidence sync
sprints.goal: Mirror the open epic in repo artifacts and record the planning-only proof for the issue-creation slice.
tasks: Update OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md and docs/PROGRESS.md for the open epic.
Create execution scope, handoff, review, validation, and closeout artifacts.
Run planning-only validation and closeout under the corrected Stage 6 contract.
acceptance_criteria: OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md and docs/PROGRESS.md reflect the open rollout epic.
The planning packet validates without downstream repo writes.
Stage 6 closeout runs in planning_only mode and does not refresh graph/wiki artifacts.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify that epic #579 and all seven downstream child issues faithfully mirror the rollout order and thin-adapter acceptance criteria approved in issues #575 and #576.
specialist_reviews.summary: Confirmed that epic #579, local-ai-machine#515, ai-integration-services#1405, seek-and-ponder#190, knocktracker#187, Stampede#195, HealthcarePlatform#1513, and ASC-Evaluator#15 now align with the governance plan and SSOT reconciliation. The only residual risk is future execution drift during downstream implementation, not issue-map inconsistency.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude Opus 4.6 returned no blocking findings. The review requested status-alignment cleanup between OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md and docs/PROGRESS.md, correction of a typo in approved_by, and completion of the expected cross-review/validation/closeout evidence. Those follow-ups were incorporated in this planning slice.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Create the governance rollout epic, open the downstream child issue map, mirror the open epic in governance repo artifacts, and capture planning-only review and validation evidence without mutating downstream repos.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Route the drafted planning packet through the governed Claude review wrapper, incorporate any required changes, and finalize validation and closeout for the epic-opening slice.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Epic #579 and all seven downstream child issues exist with the approved rollout order and repo-specific acceptance criteria.
The planning packet keeps downstream repo writes out of this branch.
Governance mirrors are updated to reflect the open rollout epic.
Alternate-family review confirms that the issue map faithfully mirrors issues #575 and #576.

material_deviation_rules: If a downstream repo needs direct edits, stop and route the work to that repo's issue lane rather than widening issue #579.
If any child issue body lacks repo-specific acceptance criteria, fix the issue map before closeout.
If alternate-family review rejects the epic-opening packet, stop and revise before publishing the planning PR.
approved_by: operator approval: Create epic, issues, ACs, fix, verify with proof, follow repo rules exactly
